questions.question: Which property is used to horizontally align text within a block element?
questions.explanation: text-align controls the horizontal alignment of inline content within a block container.

questions.question: How do you center a block element horizontally?
questions.explanation: Setting margin: 0 auto on a block element with an explicit width centers it horizontally within its parent.

questions.question: What is required for margin: auto to center a block element?
options: The element must have an explicit width
The parent must have position: relative
questions.explanation: margin: auto only works for centering when the element has an explicit width (or max-width) set.

questions.question: Which vertical-align value is the default?
questions.explanation: The default value of vertical-align is 'baseline', which aligns the element's baseline with the parent's baseline.

questions.question: What does vertical-align NOT work on?
questions.explanation: vertical-align only works on inline, inline-block, and table-cell elements. It has no effect on block-level elements.

questions.question: Which Flexbox properties center content both vertically and horizontally?
questions.explanation: align-items: center (cross axis) and justify-content: center (main axis) together center flex items both vertically and horizontally.

questions.question: What is the shorthand Grid property for centering an item?
questions.explanation: place-items: center is shorthand for align-items: center and justify-items: center, centering items in their grid cells.

questions.question: Why do you need transform: translate(-50%, -50%) when using absolute positioning for centering?
options: top: 50% centers the top edge, not the middle
questions.explanation: top: 50% positions the element's top edge at 50%. transform: translate(-50%, -50%) shifts it back by half its own size, true centering.

questions.question: Which is NOT a valid value for text-align?
questions.explanation: baseline is a vertical-align value, not a text-align value. text-align accepts left, right, center, justify, start, end.

questions.question: How do you center a single line of text vertically within a fixed-height container?
questions.explanation: Setting line-height equal to the container height vertically centers a single line of text. This works because text is vertically centered within its line height.

questions.question: What does justify-content: center do in a flex container?
options: Centers items on the cross axis
Centers items on the main axis
questions.explanation: justify-content aligns flex items along the main axis. justify-content: center centers them horizontally (or vertically if flex-direction: column).

questions.question: Which alignment method is best for centering an element of unknown size?
questions.explanation: transform: translate(-50%, -50%) with absolute positioning works regardless of the element's size, making it ideal for unknown or dynamic sizes.

questions.question: What does text-align: justify do?
options: Stretches text to fill the width
questions.explanation: text-align: justify adjusts word spacing so each line fills the full width, creating even left and right margins.

questions.question: How do you center a flex item when there's only one item?
options: align-items and justify-content on the container
questions.explanation: With a single flex item, both margin: auto on the item and align-items/justify-content on the container will center it.

questions.question: What is the default value of align-items in a flex container?
questions.explanation: The default align-items value is 'stretch', which stretches flex items to fill the container's cross axis.

questions.question: Which is an accessibility concern with text-align: justify?
options: Uneven word spacing can reduce readability
questions.explanation: Justified text can create uneven gaps between words (rivers), which may reduce readability for some users, especially those with dyslexia.

questions.question: What does align-self: center do in Flexbox?
options: Centers a single flex item on the cross axis
Centers a single item on the main axis
questions.explanation: align-self overrides align-items for a specific flex item, centering it on the cross axis independently of other items.

questions.question: What is the difference between place-items and place-content in Grid?
options: place-items aligns items within cells; place-content aligns the grid itself
questions.explanation: place-items controls alignment within grid cells, while place-content controls alignment of the entire grid within the container.

questions.question: How can you center an absolutely positioned element horizontally only?
questions.explanation: For horizontal centering of an absolute element: left: 50% positions left edge at center, transform: translateX(-50%) shifts it left by half width.

questions.question: Which method centers content both horizontally and vertically with the least code?
questions.explanation: display: grid; place-items: center; is the most concise way to center content both horizontally and vertically.
